name: 30-Day Faith Challenge
description: A month-long journey through daily scripture readings and reflective prompts to deepen your congregation's faith.
reflections: Write down three ways God has shown up for you this week.
Take 5 minutes to sit in silence and listen for God's voice.
Send an encouraging text to someone who might be struggling.
Journal about a time when God turned your weakness into strength.
Pray for someone you haven't spoken to in a while.
List five things you're grateful for right now.
Read this verse three times slowly. What stands out to you?
Share this verse with someone who needs to hear it today.
Spend 10 minutes in prayer — no requests, just praise.
Memorize today's verse and carry it with you all day.
Reflect on how this verse applies to a current challenge in your life.
Write a prayer based on today's scripture.
Think about one person who models this verse. Reach out to thank them.
How has your faith grown since you started this challenge?
What does walking by faith look like in your everyday life?
Choose one action today that reflects this scripture.
Share your favorite verse from this challenge so far.
Turn today's verse into a prayer you pray throughout the day.
Invite someone to join you for the rest of this challenge.
Look for evidence of God's faithfulness in your day today.
Write a letter to your future self about what God is teaching you.
Fast from social media for one hour and spend it in the Word.
Ask God to reveal one area where you can trust Him more.
Encourage someone today with words that reflect this verse.
What is one step of faith God is asking you to take?
Worship in a way that's outside your comfort zone today.
Read an extra chapter of the Bible today as an act of devotion.
Thank God for three prayers He has answered recently.
Share your testimony of what this challenge has meant to you.
Commit to one spiritual habit you'll continue after this challenge ends.

name: Weekly Sermon Series
description: Promote your Sunday sermons with engaging posts throughout the week — teasers, recaps, and discussion prompts.

templates:
📢 Coming this Sunday!

{seriesTitle} continues with Part {n}: {themeTitle}

{scriptureRef} reminds us: "{scriptureText}"

Invite someone to join you! 🤝

reflections: How can we find peace when the storms of life feel overwhelming?
What does it mean to truly trust God with our future?
In what ways is God calling you to step out in faith?
How do we balance planning for tomorrow while trusting God today?
What areas of your life need to be surrendered to God?
How does community strengthen our walk with God?
What does authentic worship look like in your daily life?
How can we be salt and light in our workplaces and schools?
What does it look like to love our neighbors well?
How do we handle doubt without losing faith?
What spiritual disciplines are helping you grow right now?
How do we stay rooted in God's Word during busy seasons?

name: 21-Day Prayer Journey
description: Guide your community through a focused season of prayer with daily prompts, scriptures, and reflection points.
reflections: Pray for your family — for protection, unity, and spiritual growth.
Lift up your church leaders today. Pray for wisdom and endurance.
Pray for someone who doesn't yet know Jesus. Ask God to open their heart.
Thank God for 5 specific blessings in your life right now.
Pray for your community — for safety, healing, and hope.
Ask God to reveal any unforgiveness you're holding onto.
Pray for the nations and for missionaries serving around the world.
Lift up anyone you know who is sick or struggling today.
Pray for wisdom in your decisions this week.
Spend time simply praising God — no requests, just adoration.
Pray for children and youth in your church and community.
Ask the Holy Spirit to guide your words and actions today.
Pray for your workplace — colleagues, leaders, and your witness there.
Lift up marriages and families in your church.
Pray for revival in your city and nation.
Ask God to grow your compassion for the hurting and the lost.
Pray for financial provision and generosity in your community.
Lift up those serving in the military and first responders.
Pray that God would use your church to transform your neighborhood.
Thank God for answered prayers and praise Him for what's coming.
Commit to continued prayer beyond this journey. What will you keep praying for?

name: Easter Outreach Campaign
description: Build excitement and invite your community to Easter services with a multi-week countdown, devotionals, and invitations.

templates:
This Easter, discover the hope that changes everything ✝️

Join us for a special Easter celebration.

"{scriptureText}" — {scriptureRef}

Doors open early. We saved a seat for you! 🪑

reflections: Easter reminds us that death doesn't have the final word — hope does.
The cross shows us the depth of God's love. Nothing can separate us from it.
Because He lives, we can face tomorrow with confidence.
Easter is more than a holiday — it's the foundation of our faith.
The empty tomb changes everything. What once was lost has been found.
Invite someone who might be searching for hope this Easter.
Jesus conquered the grave so we could live with purpose and joy.
The resurrection proves that God keeps every promise.
Who will you invite to experience the love of Christ this Easter?
Let's celebrate the greatest comeback in history together!
The same power that raised Jesus from the dead lives in you.
Easter is the perfect time to start fresh with God.

name: Christmas Advent Series
description: Countdown to Christmas with daily devotionals covering the themes of Hope, Peace, Joy, and Love during the Advent season.
reflections: Today we light the candle of Hope. What are you hoping for this Christmas?
The Prince of Peace was born in a humble manger. Where do you need His peace today?
Joy to the world! How can you spread joy to someone today?
God's love came down at Christmas. Who can you show love to this week?
Emmanuel — God with us. You are never alone.
The shepherds left everything to see Jesus. What might God be calling you to?
The wise men brought gifts. What gift can you offer God today?
Mary pondered these things in her heart. Take time to quietly reflect today.
A star guided the way to Jesus. How is God guiding you right now?
The angels announced good news of great joy. Share the good news with someone!
In the busyness of the season, pause to remember the reason we celebrate.
Just as the magi traveled far, our faith sometimes requires a journey of trust.
The light came into the darkness, and the darkness has not overcome it.
This Christmas, open your heart to receive the greatest gift ever given.
Unto us a child is born! Celebrate the miracle of Christ's birth today.
What gift of generosity can you give this Christmas season?
Remember those who are lonely or hurting this Christmas. Reach out with love.
The Christmas story is for everyone — who can you share it with?
Take time today to read Luke 2 as a family.
Sing a Christmas hymn and let its message fill your heart.
Reflect on God's faithfulness throughout this year.
Write a prayer of thanks for the gift of Jesus.
Christmas Eve — the wait is almost over. Prepare your heart.
Merry Christmas! He is here. Emmanuel. God with us.
As Christmas Day closes, carry the spirit of Christ with you into the new year.

name: Youth Ministry Series
description: Engaging, high-energy content designed for teens and young adults — relatable language, real-life application, and faith building.

templates:
Hey fam 👋

Week {n} of {seriesTitle}:

📖 {scriptureRef}: "{scriptureText}"

{reflection}

Drop a 🔥 if this speaks to you!

reflections: You don't have to have it all figured out. God meets you right where you are.
Being different isn't a weakness — it's your superpower in God's kingdom.
Your identity isn't defined by likes or followers. It's defined by God.
It's okay to have questions about your faith. That's how you grow.
God didn't create you to fit in. He created you to stand out.
Pressure to be perfect? Jesus already handled that. Just be real.
Your mistakes don't disqualify you from God's purpose for your life.
True friends point you towards Jesus, not away from Him.
Social media shows highlights. God sees the full story — and He still loves you.
You have something to offer this world that no one else can. Don't hold back.
It takes courage to follow Jesus when everyone else is going the other direction.
God's plans for you are bigger than anything you could scroll past online.

name: Community Outreach Campaign
description: Rally your church to serve the community with volunteer drives, event promotions, and impact stories.
reflections: This weekend: help us pack 200 food boxes for families in need. Sign up today!
Every act of kindness matters. What's one way you can serve someone today?
Volunteer spotlight: celebrating the unsung heroes in our community.
We're collecting school supplies for local families. Can you contribute?
Our neighborhood clean-up made a visible difference. Thank you to every volunteer!
Serving others isn't just something we do — it's who we are.
This Saturday: free community breakfast for anyone who needs a warm meal.
Thank you to everyone who donated! Together we raised enough to support 50 families.
Looking for ways to get involved? We have opportunities for every schedule.
When the church goes outside its walls, amazing things happen.
Our next outreach event is coming up. Bring your family and make a memory that matters.
Impact report: see what God accomplished through our community efforts this month.

name: Bible Study Series
description: Walk your community through a book of the Bible with daily or weekly study notes, discussion questions, and key insights.
reflections: What does this passage reveal about God's character?
How does this apply to a situation you're currently facing?
What promise is God making in this passage? How can you claim it?
Is there a command here that challenges your current lifestyle?
How does this passage connect to the bigger story of the Bible?
What cultural context helps us understand this verse better?
Write this verse in your own words. What does it mean to you personally?
If you could ask the author one question about this passage, what would it be?
How does this passage point to Jesus?
What practical step can you take this week based on what you've studied?
Discuss this passage with someone this week. What new insight did they share?
How has this study changed your understanding of God's Word?

name: New Believers Journey
description: A gentle, welcoming series designed for people new to faith — covering the basics of Christianity with warmth and simplicity.

templates:
Welcome to Step {n} of your {seriesTitle} 🌱

📖 {scriptureRef}: "{scriptureText}"

{reflection}

Questions? Ask in the comments — no question is too small! 🤗

reflections: Welcome! Starting this journey is the most important decision you'll ever make.
What does it mean to be 'saved'? Simply put — you are forgiven, loved, and never alone.
Prayer is just talking to God. No perfect words needed. Start a conversation today.
The Bible is God's love letter to you. Start with the book of John.
You don't have to clean yourself up before coming to God. He accepts you as you are.
Finding a church community means finding a family to grow with.
Baptism is your public declaration: I belong to Jesus now.
Doubts are normal. Bring them to God — He can handle your questions.
God's grace means you can't earn His love. It's a free gift.
Worship isn't just singing. It's living your life in gratitude for what God has done.
Start a daily Bible reading habit — even 5 minutes makes a difference.
You were created on purpose, for a purpose. Ask God to reveal it to you.

name: Gratitude & Thanksgiving Series
description: A focused series on cultivating thankfulness, perfect for the Thanksgiving season or any time your community needs a grateful-heart reset.
reflections: Name three blessings from today — no matter how small.
Write a thank-you note to someone who has impacted your life.
Thank God for something you usually take for granted.
Look around your home. What are you most grateful for?
Recall a prayer that God answered this year. Thank Him again.
Express gratitude for a challenge that helped you grow.
Who made you smile this week? Let them know you appreciate them.
Thank God for His presence — even in the hard seasons.
Share a meal with someone and express what they mean to you.
Start a gratitude journal. Write three things before bed tonight.
Thank God for His promises that never fail.
Look at your calendar this week. Which moment are you most thankful for?
Gratitude isn't just a feeling — it's a choice. Choose it today.
End this series by sharing your biggest takeaway about thankfulness.
